# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 163

This is turn 163. Turns 1 through 162 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 163.

According to a document from 2026-06-23, Turn 162 left canonical enumeration and serialization of the \(C\)-stable pairs \((Z,W)\) as the remaining compiler bottleneck. 

## Turn 163 — quadratic admissibility makes the nested-section compiler canonical

The frozen \(h_0\) submission remains unchanged: 256 bytes, SHA-256  
`4f81b04ae00e737a244f2445d70b095333a427508958f11489e3cabdc0192fdc`. 

### Quadratic admissibility theorem

Retain the active block
\[
R=\Phi(K)\le Z(K),\qquad M=K/R,\qquad T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[C]}M,
\]
where \(R\) is elementary abelian and \(K^4=1\).

Define the canonical \(C\)-equivariant quadratic map
\[
q:M\longrightarrow R,\qquad q(kR)=k^2.
\]

For \(C\)-stable subspaces \(W\le T_0\) and \(Z\le R\), consider
\[
1\longrightarrow R/Z\longrightarrow K_W/Z\longrightarrow W\longrightarrow1,
\]
where \(K_W\) is the inverse image of \(W\) in \(K\).

Then:

\[
\boxed{\ K_W/Z\to W\text{ admits a section}\iff q(W)\subseteq Z.\ }
\]

Necessity follows because the image of a section is elementary abelian, so every lifted \(w\) has square in \(Z\).

Conversely, if \(q(W)\subseteq Z\), every element of \(K_W/Z\) has square \(1\). Hence \(K_W/Z\) is elementary abelian and the sequence splits as a sequence of \(\mathbf F_2\)-vector spaces.

Moreover, the embedded inner sections are canonically parametrized by
\[
\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathbf F_2}(W,R/Z),
\]
so their exact number is
\[
\boxed{\ 2^{\dim W\,(\dim R-\dim Z)}.\ }
\]

Thus the outer search is no longer the full product of two module lattices. For each \(W\), put
\[
Q(W)=\langle q(w):w\in W\rangle.
\]
Since \(q\) is \(C\)-equivariant, \(Q(W)\) is a \(C\)-submodule of \(R\), and the admissible pairs are exactly
\[
W\in\operatorname{Sub}_C(T_0),\qquad
Z\in\operatorname{Sub}_C(R),\quad Q(W)\le Z.
\]

This is the uniform structural criterion that Turn 162’s word “admissible” was missing.

### Canonical module-lattice enumeration

I implemented Ganter’s Next-Closure algorithm for the closure operator
\[
\operatorname{cl}(S)=\mathbf F_2[C]S.
\]

Its closed sets are precisely the \(C\)-submodules, so it enumerates every member of
\(\operatorname{Sub}_C(R)\) and \(\operatorname{Sub}_C(T_0)\) exactly once in deterministic lectic order. No ambient subgroup lattice is constructed.

For each admissible pair, the compiler now:

1. chooses canonical row-reduced bases for \(Z\) and \(W\);
2. records \(Q(W)\le Z\);
3. enumerates inner sections as graphs of all maps \(W\to R/Z\);
4. applies the outer normalizer-section theorem to each resulting \(U\);
5. serializes \(Z,W,Q(W),U,J\) with membership lists and SHA-256 digests.

### Exact executable checks

The module enumerator was compared with exhaustive all-subspace filtering on 60 random modules of dimensions \(1\) through \(5\), with zero failures.

Representative module lattices were:

\[
\begin{array}{c|c}
\text{module}&\#\text{ submodules}\\ \hline
\mathbf F_2^2\text{ trivial}&5\\
\mathbf F_2^4\text{ irreducible }C_5&2\\
\mathbf F_2^3\text{ augmentation }S_4&3
\end{array}
\]

A \(C_4\) fixture directly exercised quadratic pruning: among the four naive pairs \(Z\le C_2,\ W\le C_2\), the pair \(Z=0,\ W=C_2\) was rejected because \(q(W)=R\). Exactly three admissible pairs remained.

For \(\mathrm{GL}_2(3)\), the canonical search produced only
\[
(Z,W)=(0,0),(R,0),
\]
with respectively \(8\) and \(4\) outer sections. This recovers the twelve embedded proper-image children from Turn 161 without subgroup-lattice enumeration.

For the order-\(384\) nonzero-edge fixture, the four stable pairs gave:

\[
\begin{array}{c|c|c}
\dim Z&\dim W&\text{outer-section counts}\\ \hline
0&0&16\\
0&1&0,0\\
1&0&16\\
1&1&8
\end{array}
\]

After passing to the quotient by \(R\), the relevant two rows are exactly the \(16\) children with intersection \(1\) and the \(8\) children with intersection \(T_0\) reported in Turn 162.

The order-\(320\) fixture has \(R\cong\mathbf F_2^2\) trivial and \(T_0=0\), so the canonical outer search contains exactly the five submodules of \(R\), as expected.

Five deliberate certificate corruptions—missing pair, duplicate pair, missing inner section, missing outer section, and wrong root digest—were all rejected at their intended lowest layer.

The submission continues to obey the required implicit tame relation, 2-core marking, modulus-\(M\) exponent encoding, and 5402-group verifier semantics. 

[Turn 163 executable bundle](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn163_nested_sections_bundle.zip)  
[Machine-readable report](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn163_nested_sections_report.json)  
[Frozen 256-byte submission](sandbox:/mnt/data/q2_galois_h0_submission.txt)

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved the quadratic admissibility criterion \(K_W/Z\) splits iff \(q(W)\le Z\), together with the exact inner-section count \(2^{\dim W(\dim R-\dim Z)}\). Implemented canonical Next-Closure enumeration of the \(C\)-module lattices, canonical graph sections, nested incidence serialization, and lowest-layer validation. Reproduced the \(\mathrm{GL}_2(3)\) \(8+4\) incidences and the order-\(384\) nonzero-edge \(16+8\) incidences without broad subgroup enumeration. The frozen \(h_0\) word is unchanged.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The new engine is a self-contained replacement supplement; the unavailable byte-level Turn-160 compiler cannot be patched directly, so its active-block front end still needs to be reconnected to this canonical pair/section layer.

**NEXT**: Replace the remaining intersection loop in the multiplication-table compiler by the exact search
\[
W\in\operatorname{Sub}_C(T_0),\qquad Q(W)\le Z\in\operatorname{Sub}_C(R),
\]
emit the resulting file-per-node DAG, and run the unchanged 256-byte submission. Any rejection should be descended to its lowest pair-lattice, inner-section, outer-section, source, or recurrence node before altering the word.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
